food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — spicy fried chicken burger with large fries and large soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for McSpicy Upsized Meal. FOODON_00002490 (fast food meal) is the closest defensible parent class. A more specific term for spicy fried chicken burger combo meal does not exist in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKK (chicken burger, fast food) is the closest defensible anchor for the burger component. The full upsized meal (burger + large fries + large drink) is a composite not individually coded in FoodEx2; the burger component dominates caloric and macro contribution.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps specifically to a fast food spicy chicken burger combo meal. SNOMED CT coverage for branded fast food items is absent in current release.

preparation_method: served with large fries and large carbonated soft drink

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (McSpicy burger + large fries + large soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one McSpicy spicy fried chicken burger (approximately 220 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and one large carbonated soft drink (approximately 376 g / 330 ml), totalling approximately 750 g as served.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McSpicy Burger, Large Fries, Large Coca-Cola
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (official nutrition disclosure) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for component verification
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data for the McSpicy burger, large fries, and large Coca-Cola separately. The upsized meal values are derived by summing the three components (McSpicy burger ~220 g: 490 kcal, 23 g protein, 24 g fat, 46 g carbs; large fries ~154 g: 490 kcal, 6 g protein, 23 g fat, 64 g carbs; large Coca-Cola ~376 g: 155 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 39 g carbs) and converting to per-100 g basis for the combined ~750 g meal. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken (NDB 05062), french fries (NDB 21137), and cola beverage (NDB 14400) were used to cross-validate macro ratios. Per-100 g values represent the blended composite of all three components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white wheat bread bun and french fries composite estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's McSpicy Upsized Meal as a composite. GI is estimated from the weighted carbohydrate contributions of the three components: (1) McSpicy burger bun — white wheat bread bun GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread reference); (2) large french fries — GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, McDonald's french fries entry, Atkinson et al. 2021, Diabetes Care); (3) large Coca-Cola — GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, cola beverage). Weighted average GI calculated using carbohydrate grams per component: burger bun ~30 g carbs (GI 70), fries ~64 g carbs (GI 63), cola ~39 g carbs (GI 63). Weighted GI = ((30×70)+(64×63)+(39×63)) / (30+64+39) = (2100+4032+2457)/133 = 8589/133 ≈ 65. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 45.8) / 100 ≈ 29.8, where available carbohydrate per serving = 19.87 g per 100 g × 750 g / 100 × (1 − fibre fraction) ≈ 45.8 g net. This is an analogous composite estimate; direct measurement is recommended for precision.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 1,600 mg sodium per full upsized meal serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the McSpicy Upsized Meal, as a single serving provides approximately 1,600 mg sodium, representing 70–80% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg for hypertensive adults per Singapore HPB dietary guidelines; if consumed, pair with a low-sodium diet for the remainder of the day and avoid adding condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ≈ 30 per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content from white bun, fries, and sugar-sweetened cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat the McSpicy Upsized Meal as an occasional indulgence only, as the estimated glycemic load of approximately 30 per serving — driven by the white wheat bun, deep-fried potato fries, and sugar-sweetened cola — will produce a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; substituting the cola with water or diet beverage and requesting no mayonnaise can modestly reduce the glycemic and caloric load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~13 g per meal), trans fat (~0.75 g per meal), high sodium (~1,600 mg per meal), and ultra-processed food classification
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of the McSpicy Upsized Meal, as the combination of saturated fat (~13 g), trans fat (~0.75 g), and sodium (~1,600 mg) in a single serving substantially exceeds recommended daily limits for saturated fat (less than 20 g/day) and sodium (less than 2,000 mg/day) per Singapore HPB and American Heart Association guidelines, and regular consumption of ultra-processed fast food meals is independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The McDonald's McSpicy Upsized Meal is a popular fast food combo meal sold at McDonald's Singapore restaurants, consisting of the iconic McSpicy spicy fried chicken burger served with a large portion of french fries and a large carbonated soft drink (typically Coca-Cola). The McSpicy burger, introduced in Singapore in 1999, features a deep-fried, heavily seasoned spicy chicken thigh fillet with a crispy, crunchy coating, served on a toasted sesame seed bun with shredded lettuce and mayonnaise. The chicken fillet is marinated with a proprietary spice blend that delivers a distinctly fiery heat, making it one of the most recognisable and beloved fast food items in Singapore. The upsized meal upgrades the standard medium fries and drink to large portions, significantly increasing the total caloric and macronutrient load. The full upsized meal weighs approximately 750 g and delivers approximately 1,145 kcal, 29 g protein, 47 g fat, and 149 g carbohydrates per serving. The meal is certified Halal by MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura), making it accessible to Muslim consumers. From a clinical nutrition perspective, the McSpicy Upsized Meal is a high-calorie, high-sodium, high-saturated-fat, and high-glycemic-load meal that is inappropriate for frequent consumption by individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycemic index of the composite meal is approximately 65 (medium), with a glycemic load of approximately 30 per serving, driven primarily by the refined carbohydrates in the white wheat bun, deep-fried potato fries, and sugar-sweetened cola. The meal is ultra-processed and contains multiple allergens including gluten, egg, sesame, soy, and milk. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following a gluten-free diet.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a McSpicy Upsized Meal?
Is the McSpicy Upsized Meal halal?

How much fat is in the McSpicy Upsized Meal?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived by summing McDonald's Singapore's publicly disclosed per-item nutrition data for the McSpicy burger, large fries, and large Coca-Cola, then converting to a per-100 g basis for the composite meal. McDonald's Singapore nutrition data is an official brand disclosure but is not an independent laboratory-verified entry in a primary nutrition database such as HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. USDA SR Legacy was used for cross-validation of macro ratios. GI is an analogous composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are calculated as a weighted composite of three components: McSpicy burger (~220 g), large fries (~154 g), and large Coca-Cola (~376 g), totalling ~750 g. Per-100 g values reflect the blended average of the full meal. 2.
